structured_payload_candidate.title: Cooling Tower Fault Conditions
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Fault conditions include: tower fan has failed, inlet end switch indicates valve not open 90 seconds after command, or outlet end switch indicates valve not open 90 seconds after command.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Generate Level 2 alarm and trigger response.
structured_payload_candidate.fault_condition: Tower fan failed; inlet isolation valve not open 90 sec after command; outlet isolation valve not open 90 sec after command.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: iv. Cooling Towers (a) Tower fan has failed as defined above, or (b) Inlet end switch indicates valve is not open 90 seconds after valve is commanded open, or (c) Outlet end switch indicates valve is not open 90 seconds after valve is commanded open.

evidence_quote: iv. Cooling Towers (a) Tower fan has failed as defined above, or (b) Inlet end switch indicates valve is not open 90 seconds after valve is commanded open, or (c) Outlet end switch indicates valve is not open 90 seconds after valve is commanded open.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Response to Fault for Fans, Pumps, and Cooling Towers
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Upon identification of a fault condition: the next commanded off equipment in the staging order shall be commanded on while alarming equipment remains commanded on. If the next equipment also fails, repeat until required quantity is proven on or no more equipment available. Set alarming equipment to last positions in staging order reverse chronologically. Non-alarming equipment follow even wear logic. Equipment in alarm shall run if called for by staging order.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Fault condition identified on fan, pump, or cooling tower
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Command next equipment on; if it fails, repeat; move alarming equipment to last position; non-alarming equipment follow even wear; alarming equipment runs if called.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: 2. Upon identification of a fault condition: i. For fans, pumps, and cooling towers: (a) The next commanded off equipment in the staging order, Equipment 'B', shall be commanded on while alarming Equipment 'A' remains commanded on. (b) If Equipment 'B' fails to prove status (i.e., it also goes into alarm), it shall remain commanded on and the preceding step shall be repeated until the quantity of equipment called for by the current stage has proven on, or there are no more available equipment. (c) Set alarming equipment to the last positions in the lead/lag or lead/standby staging order sequenced reverse chronologically (i.e., the equipment that alarmed most recently is sent to last position). (d) Staging order of non-alarming equipment shall follow the even wear logic. Equipment in alarm can only automatically move up on the staging order if another equipment goes into alarm. (e) Equipment in alarm shall run if so called for by the lead/lag or lead/standby staging order and present stage.

evidence_quote: 2. Upon identification of a fault condition: i. For fans, pumps, and cooling towers: (a) The next commanded off equipment in the staging order, Equipment 'B', shall be commanded on while alarming Equipment 'A' remains commanded on. (b) If Equipment 'B' fails to prove status (i.e., it also goes into alarm), it shall remain commanded on and the preceding step shall be repeated until the quantity of equipment called for by the current stage has proven on, or there are no more available equipment. (c) Set alarming equipment to the last positions in the lead/lag or lead/standby staging order sequenced reverse chronologically (i.e., the equipment that alarmed most recently is sent to last position). (d) Staging order of non-alarming equipment shall follow the even wear logic. Equipment in alarm can only automatically move up on the staging order if another equipment goes into alarm. (e) Equipment in alarm shall run if so called for by the lead/lag or lead/standby staging order and present stage.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Response to Fault for Chillers and Boilers
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Upon identification of a fault condition: the next commanded off equipment shall be commanded on while alarming equipment is commanded off and set to last position. If the next equipment also fails, repeat until quantity proven on or all equipment tried. If all tried and non-alarming equipment less than called for, the most recently alarmed equipment remains commanded on. Non-alarming equipment follow even wear logic. Equipment in alarm shall run if called for by staging order.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Fault condition identified on chiller or boiler
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Command next equipment on; if it fails, repeat; move alarming equipment to last position; if all tried and insufficient, most recently alarmed remains on; non-alarming follow even wear; alarming runs if called.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: ii. For chillers and boilers: (a) The next commanded off equipment in the staging order, Equipment 'B', shall be commanded on while alarming Equipment 'A' is commanded off and set to the last position in the lead/lag staging order. (b) If Equipment B fails to prove status (i.e., it also goes into alarm), repeat the preceding step until the quantity of equipment called for by the lead/lag logic have proven on or until all equipment has been tried. (c) If all equipment has been tried and the quantity of non-alarming equipment is less than called for then the most recently alarmed equipment will remain commanded on. (d) Staging order of non-alarming equipment shall follow the even wear logic. Equipment in alarm can only automatically move up in the staging order if another equipment goes into alarm. (e) Equipment in alarm shall run if so called for by the lead/lag staging order and present stage.

(b) If Equipment B fails to prove status (i.e., it also goes into alarm), repeat the preceding step until the quantity of equipment called for by the lead/lag logic have proven on or until all equipment has been tried. (c) If all equipment has been tried and the quantity of non-alarming equipment is less than called for then the most recently alarmed equipment will remain commanded on. (d) Staging order of non-alarming equipment shall follow the even wear logic. Equipment in alarm can only automatically move up in the staging order if another equipment goes into alarm. (e) Equipment in alarm shall run if so called for by the lead/lag staging order and present stage.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Hand Operation Detection for Fans and Pumps
structured_payload_candidate.summary: If a piece of equipment is ON-in-hand, the equipment shall be set to the lead device, and a Level 4 alarm shall be generated. Hand operation is determined by status point not matching its ON/OFF point for 15 seconds after a time delay of 60 seconds when the equipment is commanded OFF.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Equipment commanded OFF but status indicates ON
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Set equipment to lead device, generate Level 4 alarm.
structured_payload_candidate.fault_condition: Status point not matching its ON/OFF point for 15 seconds after a time delay of 60 seconds when the equipment is commanded OFF.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: If a piece of equipment is ON-in-hand (e.g., via an HOA switch or local control of VFD), the equipment shall be set to the lead device, and a Level 4 alarm shall be generated. ... 1. Fans and Pumps i. Status point not matching its ON/OFF point for 15 seconds after a time delay of 60 seconds when the equipment is commanded OFF.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Trim & Respond Logic Execution
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Upstream system/plant setpoint controlled by T&R loop
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Trim & Respond logic shall reset the setpoint within the range SPmin to SPmax. When the associated device is OFF, the setpoint shall be SP0. The reset logic shall be active while the associated device is proven ON, starting Td after initial device start command. When active, every time step T, if R≤I, trim the setpoint by SPtrim. If R > I, respond by changing the setpoint by SPres*(R – I), but no more than SPres-max.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Device proven ON and Td elapsed
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Every time step T: if R≤I, trim by SPtrim; if R>I, respond by SPres*(R-I) but no more than SPres-max.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Trim & Respond logic shall reset the setpoint within the range SPmin to SPmax. When the associated device is OFF, the setpoint shall be SP0. The reset logic shall be active while the associated device is proven ON, starting Td after initial device start command. When active, every time step T, if R≤I, trim the setpoint by SPtrim. If there are more than I requests, respond by changing the setpoint by SPres*(R – I), (i.e., the number of requests minus the number of ignored requests) but no more than SPres-max.

evidence_quote: See Section 5.1.14.4  for an example of T&R implementation.
